product_results.title: Sherpa Original Deluxe Travel Pet Carrier, Airline Approved & Guaranteed On Board - Charcoal Gray, Medium

details.heading: Ships in product packaging
details.content: This item has been tested to certify it can ship safely in its original box or bag to avoid unnecessary packaging. Since 2015, we have reduced the weight of outbound packaging per shipment by 41% on average, that’s over 2 million tons of packaging material. Learn more

buy_used.caption: Used - Like New

about_item: AIRLINE APPROVED: The Sherpa Original Deluxe Pet Carrier is airline approved and part of our Guaranteed On Board program, which provides comfort and peace of mind to pets and their owners; it's perfect for traveling by air, bus, car, or train

IN-CABIN FRIENDLY: Our patented spring wire frame allows the rear end of the carrier to be pushed down several inches to easily fit under airplane seats in compliance with most major airline requirements and FAA regulations

PERFECT FOR TRAVEL: Features mesh panels for ventilation, escape-proof locking zippers, top and side entries for easy and comfortable loading, waterproof interior base, and a removable machine washable cozy base liner

VERSATILE DESIGN: Can be carried by hand with the padded top handle, or over the shoulder using the included detachable carrying strap and integrated metal D-rings - whichever suits your comfort and convenience

MULTIFUNCTIONAL: The pet carrier can also be used a soft-sided crate for regular outings and vet visits with cats and small dogs; includes a large storage pocket, and a luggage strap that doubles as a seatbelt security pass-through for car rides
PRODUCT DETAILS: Charcoal Gray, Medium, 17" x 11" x 10.5"; fits pets up to 16 pounds

CHOOSING THE RIGHT SIZE: Choose the perfect carrier size based on your pet's measurements, followed by their weight; make sure not to exceed the maximum weight limit, and allow enough space for your pet to change positions inside the carrier

features.title: Original Deluxe Pet Carrier
features.text: The Original Deluxe Pet Carrier is an airline approved carrier that lets your pet travel comfortably, whether you are on the go, in your car, or talking a walk. Easy to use and durable enough to keep your pets contained and protected.

features.title: Fits Under Most Airline Seats
features.text: The spring-wire frame allows the rear end of the carrier to be pushed down several inches to fit under most airline seats in compliance with airline requirements and FAA regulations, designating the pet carrier as in-cabin friendly* and airline approved.

features.title: Multiple Features
features.text: Features included are mesh panels for ventilation, top and side entries, locking zippers for added safety, and a seatbelt safety strap.

features.title: Guaranteed on Board
features.text: Working with major airlines, we made sure to provide you and your pet with a carrier that is Guaranteed On Board*. The G.O.B. program provides all the information you will need for your future travels with your pet.

features.title: Folds Flat
features.text: The carrier is collapsible for extra convenience, allowing for easy storage and portability.

product_details.warranty_description: Sherpa® One-Year Limited Warranty: Sherpa® warrants that for a period of one year from the date of original retail purchase, this product will be free from defects in material and workmanship. This warranty does not cover normal wear and tear, damage such as chewing or scratching caused by pets, misuse of the product, or use contrary to the operating instructions. Please retain proof of purchase; proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. If Sherpa® determines a new product to be an appropriate replacement, it will be made with a similar product of equal or greater value. This warranty is valid for the original retail purchaser from the date of the initial retail purchase and is not transferable. The aforementioned Limited Warranty is the only warranty offered and Sherpa® disclaims all other warranties, expressed or implied (including, without limitation, the warranties of merchantability and of fitness for a particular purpose). Additionally, Sherpa® disclaims any and all liability for consequential damages resulting from the use or ownership of this product.

summary.text: Customers find the pet carrier to be well-made, sturdy, and comfortable, with a sherpa lining that makes it suitable for travel. The carrier fits well under seats, particularly in Delta's 757-200, and works well for various uses, including long road trips and flying Allegiant. The size receives mixed feedback, with some finding it perfect while others say it's too small, and the zipper durability is also mixed with reports of faulty zippers and torn ones with minimal use.

authors_reviews.title: American Airlines & Delta: No Problems
authors_reviews.text: I reviewed another style of this same sherpa carrier--it's my 'go to' since I know that the two airlines I have flown with this carrier I've never had problems. On Delta and American Airlines, the carrier with Bear inside fits under the seat in front of me. A few trips ago, I began buying the upgraded seats. It costs me more but it's worth it. There's more legroom and as well I can board quickly and leave quickly, which helps when carrying a dog.I always buy a medium. Small of course too small for my dog. But the large is too big to fit under the seats--just note this that American Airline (and maybe Delta too) has limitations on carry one dog carrier size, so the medium is as large as you can go, at least with these Sherpa styles. A medium just fits my 12-14 pound chihuahua mix. Not the usual tiny chihuahua - he is beefy! He can stand up, turn around, and does well in this carrier. This one has more ventilation so that's a plus. There's only one pocket, which the other style I used had two, so I wish there were another pocket.I ordered one of those soft straps that you can pull wide to fit on your shoulder more comfortably. Carrying around a 12-14 pound dog doesn't sound like a lot but it can be awkward and heavy hurrying what seems like miles through an airport. It does help, though it's not ideal. Made by the same company.I did fill out the form on guaranteed travel for the other style I had and likely will do it with this one. Never had to use it so no idea if they really do honor it.Zips at the top. Zips at the front. The bedding part is thin but it works and he's pretty comfortable in there--he has taken naps in the carrier if I have it out before a trip, or if I need to use it for vet trips.This is a 'no frills' carrier - it's plain, it's simple, but I like it because I know the airlines I fly accept it and it will fit under the seats. The price is reasonable as well.
authors_reviews.author: Kathryn Magendie

authors_reviews.title: This carrier is as well made as the one I bought 18 yrs ago!
authors_reviews.text: This Pet Carrier, which I use for my two Burmese/Tonkinese kittens, is exactly like the first carrier I bought and used for eighteen years for my first two Burmese cats. Because they are small cats, they both can fit into the carrier together, even as adults. I just needed a second one because they were being altered and had to be brought home in separate carriers. This one is the same excellent quality as the first. It is very comfortable and these kittens actually liked sleeping and playing inside of it when it arrived and I kept it in their playroom for a few months because they enjoyed it so much. These carriers clean up beautifully and I also put a pad in the bottom of them for added comfort, although it's not actually needed. The cats like them because they can see out of them and know that I am nearby, which is very reassuring to them. I highly recommend them because they wear so well!
authors_reviews.author: Kathleen

authors_reviews.title: Zeca loves it! 🐾
authors_reviews.text: this is by far the best carrier we got for our Zeca! he fits nicely in there and he loves to be in it in public transport and on the plane! he can’t really stand as he has long legs, but so far no trouble with any airlines!quality is great compared to another one we had from an Amazon brand.i recommend to train your dog before in different scenarios and getting them in so they’re comfortable when traveling.
authors_reviews.author: Lukas Elsinger

authors_reviews.title: This is a repeat purchase, which probably says it all.
authors_reviews.text: I had been reading reviews and looking for a carrier when a vet emergency mandated an immediate local purchase. I found this carrier in a local feed store, and it was the only one that had the features I was looking for--manageable size (big enough for cats, small enough to carry), structured enough to not envelope an animal, but with the flexibility to get squished a bit to fit in unyielding spaces.I bought this (for substantially more than they sell on Amazon) and have been happy with the purchase. More to the point, the cats are happy with the carrier. I leave it open in the living room in a place they associate with play, and I often find them resting or sleeping in the carriers.I have three cats, and the two smaller (10 lbs each) cats have made vet trips in one carrier. The sturdy clip-on strap hooks over the headrest in the car, and I clip the seat belt around the strap (not the carrier body).The base has a velcro'd-in, removable fabric-covered board that can be taken out to clean, and a soft sherpa-covered removable pad for washing. I bought a two-pack of spare pads. These are machine washable, but come out a bit roughened up--I'd recommend giving them a hand wash unless they get dire. And the replacements are inexpensive enough to dispose of a badly-soiled pad if that seems necessary.These collapse flat for storage if desired. I'm just leaving them out for the girls to hang in so that a trip is less traumatic. Seems to work. I didn't hear a peep out of them during the last trip in. (Getting two cats in one carrier, however, was a bit of a clown act. I recommend getting both cats and the carrier in a small room with doors shut before you begin.I think my earlier shopping difficulty was trying to find something that would meet all my possible carrier needs, trying to find something convenience for a vet run AND comfortable for international travel with long layovers. Necessity taught me that the reality is we'll need this for short local runs much more often than longer trips, and if I need to, I can get something different. For today, this ticks all the boxes.If you're in the same quandary I was in, I can tell you that these are well-made, seem very affordable, and the cats are so comfortable in them they hang out there. Seemingly no negative associations. I have different colors in the event that I have to travel with three cats, so I can sort out who is where at a glance.
authors_reviews.author: snowleopard
